Fixes Synapse-Admin failing with "Server communication error" when the admin account already existed.

## Fixed
- **`ADMIN_USER` now promotes an *existing* account to server admin.** The admin bootstrap only set the server-admin flag when it *created* the account (`register_new_matrix_user -a`); if the account already existed — e.g. you registered it in Element first — it was left as a normal user. Synapse-Admin needs a Synapse **server admin** (not the same as an Element *room* admin), so it loaded but every data request failed with **"Server communication error"** (the `/_synapse/admin` API returns 403). The bootstrap now promotes the existing account directly in PostgreSQL (the documented way to grant server admin) — set `ADMIN_USER`/`ADMIN_PASSWORD` to that account and restart. It only sets the admin flag; it does not change the password. Init also no longer skips the admin step when an earlier run left a marker file, so re-setting `ADMIN_USER` on an existing install is enough to promote. Fail-safe: retries on the next restart if Postgres isn't reachable yet.

Thanks to the reporter in #6.
